ace: noun. A playing card showing a single pip, typically the highest or lowest ranking card in a game.
acne: noun. A skin condition, usually of the face, that is common in adolescents.
affine: adjective. Assigning finite values to finite quantities.
ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
caffeine: noun. An alkaloid, C 8 H 10 N 4 O 2, found naturally in tea and coffee plants, which acts as a mild stimulant on the central nervous system.
cane: noun. A plant with simple stems, like bamboo or sugar cane, or the stem thereof: The slender, flexible main stem of a plant such as bamboo,
cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
eff: noun. Alternative spelling of ef; the name of the Latin script letter F / f.
efface: verb. To erase (as anything impressed or inscribed upon a surface); to render illegible or indiscernible.
enface: verb. To write or print on the face of (a draft, bill, etc.).
face: noun. The front part of the head of a human or other animal, featuring the eyes, nose, and mouth, and the surrounding area.
faience: noun. A type of tin - glazed earthenware ceramic, used domestically for tableware and in architecture as a decorative material.
fain: adjective. Often followed by of: glad, well-pleased.
fan: noun. A hand-held device consisting of concertinaed material, or slats of material, gathered together at one end, that may be opened out
fane: noun. A weathercock, a weather vane.
fee: noun. An amount charged for a privilege.
fen: noun. A type of wetland fed by ground water and runoff, containing peat below the waterline, characteristically alkaline.
fence: noun. A thin artificial barrier that separates two pieces of land or forms a perimeter enclosing the lands of a house, building, etc.
fice: noun. Alternative form of feist (“a feist dog”).
fie: interjection. Sometimes followed by on or upon: used to express distaste, disgust, or outrage.
fief: noun. Land held of a superior, particularly on condition of homage, fealty, and personal service, especially military service.
fife: noun. A small shrill pipe, resembling the piccolo flute, used chiefly to accompany the drum in military music
fin: noun. One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre / maneuver.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
niece: noun. A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother (" fraternal niece "), or of
ean: verb. To bring forth young; to yean.
feni: noun. An alcoholic drink made in Goa, India, from fermented cashew apples or coconut toddy (sap).
finca: noun. A country estate, farm or ranch in Spain or Hispanic America.
inca: noun. Any of several species of hummingbirds in the genus Coeligena
naff: adjective. Bad; tasteless, poorly thought out, not workable.
nef: noun. An extravagant table ornament and container used in the Middle Ages and Renaissance, made in the shape of a ship.
nief: noun. A serf or bondsman born into servitude.
nife: noun. The innermost layers or core of the Earth, composed chiefly of nickel and iron.
